title: Cleome heterochroma

herb_names_common.name: mexican clover (cleome heterophylla is sometimes confused with cleome heterochroma, hence the name 'mexican clover' is sometimes used)

uses: Cleome heterochroma, also known as the yellow bee flower, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. In traditional medicine, Cleome heterochroma is used to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts are also used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as an antispasmodic. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cleome heterochroma for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Cleome heterochroma is classified as a species of flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family, which is a family of flowering plants. This family is part of the Brassicales order within the Brassicales clade. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions and has been found in various countries, including Brazil and Peru. Cleome heterochroma belongs to the genus Cleome, which comprises around 180 species of flowering plants. The plant's classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other species in the Cleomaceae family.

The plant's extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The plant's leaves and flowers are also used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. intro: Cleome heterochroma, also known as the 'Wedding Candles' or 'Spider Flower', is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its unique, tall spikes of small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome heterochroma include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cleome heterochroma is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
